topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The knock on the door made me jump. I hadn't expected anything. Peering through the peephole, my breath hitched. It was a box, taped up with faded brown paper, sitting squarely on my welcome mat. My heart started doing a frantic little tap dance against my ribs. Who would send something to me? I fumbled with the deadbolt, my fingers clumsy with anticipation. When I finally wrestled the door open, the sight inside made me gasp. It was a collection of vintage vinyl records, clearly something the previous occupant had been hoarding. A wide, slow smile spread across my face, and I let out a soft, delighted chuckle.

[story 2]

I found the package tucked behind the overgrown hydrangea bush. It was addressed to 'Apartment 3B'—the unit I’d just moved into. A jolt of pure excitement shot through me. I snatched it up, the cardboard feeling surprisingly heavy in my hand. My eyes scanned the label, and then I saw it: a box of rare botanical seeds. My jaw dropped. I turned the package over, tracing the embossed lettering with my fingertip. A bubbling sense of possibility filled my chest, making me bounce on the balls of my feet. I practically skipped back to my apartment, already imagining the vibrant garden I could cultivate.

[story 3]

The delivery person had left the parcel leaning against the railing outside my window. It was addressed to the former resident. I approached it cautiously, my gaze fixed on the plain, unmarked brown paper. As I carefully slid the tape away, the scent of aged paper and something faintly floral wafted out. I cracked the seal, and inside, nestled in tissue paper, was a beautifully bound, leather-cased journal filled with handwritten poetry. My hands trembled slightly as I lifted it out. A warmth bloomed in my chest, spreading outward. I couldn't stop staring at the elegant script, a profound sense of joy washing over me, making me lean against the window frame, completely captivated.
